result.summary: PactFlow exposes a tenant-scoped REST hypermedia Developer API and can also be integrated via Pact Broker/Pact CLIs using the same PactFlow API token.
result.description: PactFlow is a hosted contract testing platform built on the Pact Broker, used to publish, verify, and manage API and event contracts across teams. It provides tooling for contract management, deployment checks, permissions, webhooks, and related workflow automation.

pactflow_api_token.label: PactFlow API token
pactflow_api_token.setup: Sign in to your PactFlow tenant at `https://{account}.pactflow.io`, then open **Settings → API Tokens** in the PactFlow UI and create or copy a token. Use a read-only token for `GET` access and a read-write token for non-`GET` operations. The product docs note that to publish or verify contracts you use one of the bearer tokens from the API Tokens section of your Contract Testing settings page; the Developer API docs call this a Bearer token included as `Authorization: Bearer {API_TOKEN}`. See [API Tokens](https://support.smartbear.com/swagger/contract-testing/docs/en/user-guide/user-interface/user-interface-2-x-x/settings/api-tokens.html), [Getting Started with Swagger Contract Testing](https://support.smartbear.com/swagger/contract-testing/docs/en/user-guide/getting-started-with.html), and [Authentication and Authorization](https://developer.smartbear.com/pactflow/default/authentication-and-authorization).

requiredHeaders.name: Accept
requiredHeaders.description: The getting started guide says to specify the primary content type as `application/hal+json` in the `Accept` header of all HTTP requests.

variables.name: account
variables.description: Your unique account URL; if you log in to `https://xyz.pactflow.io`, then that is the API endpoint host.
